entry.title: Re: Workflow approval history no longer visible
entry.content: I assume because it is not a bug. Admins can still view the approval history via "Manage Workflow Requests". The workaround would be to switch the Latest View and History UI off as described in the linked KBA.

entry.title: Re: SAP BAS Deployment Issue - Request for Support
entry.content: Per R3nwo comment: Hii, I am facing the same problem. My workaround was that I used the UI wizard (press F1 and search for Deployment) to create the deploy config. Basically, the UI Wizard bypasses the exact - text based prompt mechanism and the specific Type Error [ERR_INVALID_ARG_TARGET] crash happens because the node.js process in the terminal chokes on how the error return string 'ERR_BAD_REQUEST'. The Graphical UI extension handles the state management and backend request via the IDE's build-in VS Code/BAS platform API, which process and responds completely different that do not trigger this text-argument routine. So per R3nwo, this UI wizard has direct active access to the session's BTP space and authentication without manually bridging an external terminal process.

entry.title: What’s New in SAP Central Business Configuration 2604 FD1
entry.content:
With the latest release for SAP Central Business Configuration, an exciting new feature is available. This blog post will provide you with a comprehensive overview of it.

Partner Add-On Showcase Tenants Now Available with Demo Data

Add-on developers will benefit from a new option in the Deployment Target activity. By default, the Partner Add-On Showcase tenants come with demo data now. If needed, you can switch off demo data before assignment. If SAP implementation partners have the required license, they can assign the following deployment targets to their evaluation workspaces, with or without demo data:

  • Partner Add-On Showcase Development Tenant
  • Partner Add-On Showcase Customizing Tenant

Demo data selection in the Assign Deployment Target activity

For a quick summary, check out this video about what’s new. Don’t forget to also use the What’s New Viewer on the SAP Help Portal, which provides further details and links about all the recent changes.

That wraps up the new features this time. Did you like this blog? If so, please leave us a thumbs up at the top. Also, make sure to follow the SAP Central Business Configuration tag or follow this dedicated blog collection to stay up to date with the latest blog posts.

Do you have any ideas on how to further enhance SAP Central Business Configuration? Go to the Customer Influence platform to submit an improvement request or vote for the ideas of others. This helps us understand your requirements even better and prioritize our work accordingly. We’re looking forward to your improvement requests.

entry.title: Re: SAP Central Business Configuration – Blog Collection
entry.content: New blog alert − Check out our latest blog post: "What's New in SAP Central Business Configuration 2604 FD1"

entry.title: Re: SAP HANA and Hadoop Integration: ETL vs. SDA vs. SDI vs. HANA Vora
entry.content:
Current Environment and Request for Recommendations

We currently maintain two SAP HANA 2.x instances that support our Development/QA and Production environments.

Our data integration process relies on SAP Data Services to connect remotely to an Oracle database. We regularly receive Oracle Data Pump export files containing data warehouse tables sourced from HR systems. These exports are imported into an intermediate Oracle database, which serves as a bridge between the source system and SAP HANA.

To load data into SAP HANA, we currently use a combination of third-party PL/SQL-based processes and SAP HANA import utilities. Large fact tables are loaded through specialized processes, while small and medium-sized tables (typically less than one million rows) are loaded using bulk insert operations. Data is refreshed and loaded into SAP HANA six days per week.

In addition to scheduled loads, we frequently receive requests from business offices to load additional data into SAP HANA for reporting purposes. We use a SAP HANA import tool to load the data and then perform transformations and reformatting as needed. Stored procedures are extensively used to complete data transformations, load data into SAP HANA, and generate output files that are shared with other organizations in text file format.

One of our most cumbersome tasks involves processing files received in PDF and Excel (.xlsx) formats. These files must be manually reformatted into delimited text files before they can be processed by our SAP HANA import tool. This manual effort is time-consuming and prone to errors.

We are also experiencing performance challenges with our daily Oracle-to-SAP HANA data migration process. The volume of data continues to grow at approximately one gigabyte per year, and the current migration process takes approximately four hours to complete. The workflow currently involves exporting data from the source Oracle database, importing it into an intermediate Oracle database, and then loading it into SAP HANA. This multi-step process introduces complexity and additional processing time.

We are seeking recommendations to modernize and redesign our architecture. Specifically, we would like guidance on:

  1. Leveraging SAP HANA-native tools to load and transform data more efficiently.
  2. Eliminating unnecessary intermediate technologies and reducing the dependency on the bridge Oracle database.
  3. Identifying ETL solutions that can automate the conversion of PDF, Excel, and other file formats into SAP HANA-ready data structures.
  4. Improving overall load performance and reducing processing time.
  5. Supporting the ingestion and processing of unstructured data and streaming data sources.
  6. Incorporating Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning capabilities into our data platform.
  7. Establishing a scalable architecture that can support future growth and increasing data volumes.

We would appreciate recommendations for an end-to-end redesign that simplifies the current architecture, improves performance, reduces manual effort, and positions the platform for future AI and advanced analytics initiatives.

entry.title: Testing Data Flows in S/4HANA Migrations: Why SIT is Your Data Quality Filter
entry.content:
Introduction

If you are a data engineer, you probably spend months building perfect pipelines and setting strict governance rules. But how do you know they actually work when systems start talking to each other? That is what System Integration Testing (SIT) is for.

Think of SIT as the first time we turn on the switches and let data flow end-to-end across different platforms. It is not just a software check; it is the ultimate stress-test to see if your clean data stays clean when it leaves your pipeline and enters a live business environment.

When executing System Integration Testing (SIT), teams often fall into the trap of only validating the business process—checking if system A successfully passes a message to system B. But a successful technical handshake doesn't mean the data itself is sound. This is where the critical angle of data quality and integrity comes in; to prove that, let us first remember the definitions of data quality and data integrity.

Data quality definition

Data quality refers to how relevant and reliable your data is for its intended purpose. It defines whether information can be trusted and effectively applied in daily operations or advanced data analytics. True data quality also depends on preserving business semantics, which are the shared definitions, context, and meaning behind the data.

And what is Data Integrity

is about ensuring that data remains accurate, complete, and consistent as it moves across systems throughout its lifecycle. It’s not just about a single field looking right; it’s about maintaining the logical relationships, financial meaning, and correctness of the data as it flows from one system to another.

Process vs. Data

If a pipeline moves information from one system to another without crashing, a basic technical check will mark the test as "passed."

But what is actually inside that data?

This is where true data integrity comes in. Basic data quality only checks if a field looks right — like making sure a phone number has enough digits. Data integrity goes deeper than that. It's about whether the data keeps its real meaning and logic as it moves from system to system. During SIT, you'll almost always find problems that simple checks completely miss: empty fields, cut-off text, duplicate records that suddenly appear right after a migration load. If your tests only check whether the data moved, you're missing the more important information — whether it is actually usable or not.

The SAP Chain Reaction: A Lesson for Data Teams.

For data engineers, in SAP projects, weak data rules turn visible very fast, and the consequences spread quickly.

Take a simple buying flow as an example. Data moves from a legacy system into SAP Materials Management (MM), then passes into Financial Accounting (FI). If a tax code or currency field is missing in the master data, the system might still let the Goods Receipt post in MM without any errors, but the moment that same data hits the FI module, everything stops — the accounts don't balance, and the transaction fails.

This is the part that trips up a lot of teams. When a transaction fails in SIT, the first instinct is to raise it as a software bug and log a ticket. More often than not, though, it's a data governance gap hiding behind a technical error. The system didn't break — the data was never ready to begin with. For data teams, these failures show you exactly where ownership and validation rules are missing, often more clearly than any audit ever could.

A Live Feedback Loop for Data Engineers

Data engineers shouldn't wait until System Integration Test to find out their pipelines have holes in them — but SIT is usually where it happens anyway. So the smarter move is to treat it as exactly what it is: a live report card.

When you transform legacy data into a new SAP structure, SIT is the environment that proves whether your transformation logic holds up under real business transactions — not just in a dev sandbox, but end-to-end. For data scientists especially, seeing broken or missing data at this stage is a critical early warning. If a pipeline is letting bad data through now, any dashboard or model built on top of it will produce bad output. It just quietly makes your analytics wrong until someone notices.

SIT catches these broken pipelines early. That's the point. Long before they cause damage in production.

Conclusion: Working Together for Clean Data

No matter how well a system is configured, it will stall if the data running through it isn't clean. Getting successfully to UAT requires data creators and data validators to actually work together — not hand off a ticket and move on.

SIT shouldn't be treated as a way to patch quick errors and close testing tickets. When a data issue surfaces, engineers and testers need to sit together and trace it back to the root cause in the pipeline — not just fix the symptom. That's the difference between clearing a defect and actually building something reliable.

References & Further Reading

  • SAP PRESS Book: SAP S/4HANA Cloud: An Introduction (Chapter on Data Migration Workstreams and SAP Activate Testing Methodology).
  • Industry Standard Framework: The DAMA Guide to the Data Management Body of Knowledge (DAMA-DMBOK) – Section on Data Quality and Integration Governance.
  • SAP Community Guide: Data Migration in SAP S/4HANA – The Core Principles of Data Cleansing during the Realize Phase.

entry.title: Re: SAP HCM Payroll Processing & SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central Payroll - Discussions

Thanks for your question. 

We’ll do our best to keep you updated on the most important changes in this group. 
For the time being, to be aware of the last updates, the best way is to go to the Notes catalog and do a search like this

https://me.sap.com/mynotes/search/%7B%22componentsStartWith%22%3A%5B%22PY-XX-PYP%22%5D%7D 

Please note that both PCC versions are merged there as well.

entry.title: Re: Bonus Assignment Statements-Custom Fields

entry.title: Re: Downloading Your Business Configuration Adaptations in SAP Central Business Configuration
entry.content:
Hi Chirag Kothari,

Thanks for your question! No, only the adapted configuration values, which are downloaded per configuration activity. However, if you feel like a feature is missing that would help you with your work in SAP Central Business Configuration, please do not hesitate to submit an improvement request on the Customer Influence platform here: https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#/campaign/3507

Hope that helps.

Best regards, 
Fabian

entry.title: Re: Introduction to Next-Gen SAP Ariba

Navigating next-gen SAP Ariba: Product and partner licensing update (Partner Portal)

Partner session conducted on May 28, 2026 covered details on roadmap of Next-gen SAP Ariba solutions. It also covered TDD licensing and provisioning details.
This PPT captures those highlights - Navigating next-gen SAP Ariba: Product and partner licensing update

Details of this and other sessions conducted so far for Partners along with enablement materials is all available on this single page: Partner Portal - Next-gen SAP Ariba.

entry.title: Re: Joule – Getting Started with Document Grounding - setup guide - 1
entry.content: Hi Nagesh, Can you please confirm the refresh will take in account (and forget) information in a deleted document? or in an updated one... in order tu ensure only reply with the current information and not with the old one. Thanks!!!!! Manuel.

entry.title: Re: SAP Datasphere: Data Tiering Strategy and Best Practices
entry.content:
@mandli_girish 

Compute is consumed for data merging, optimizing, vacuuming, transformation flows, and all other tasks that change data. This means for frequent data change + frequent replication needs then this pattern is not ideal.  

Ideal scenarios:

  • High volume + Low/moderate update.
  • Mostly batch processing (ingest → transform → aggregate) i.e. every 1hr, 4hr, daily etc.

Inefficient when:

  • Small and frequent updates to high volume tables.

cold‑first is efficient when mostly append‑only and batch‑processed.

Hope this help. Happy learning, Jeet

entry.title: How to find version of sap ETD( SAP enterprise threat detection) in sap netweaver 758 SP01
entry.content:
Hi All,

We are working on POC for SAP EDT cloud version with direct integration from SAP BTP to our RISE sap system. , While we try to configure SAP ETD in SAP system we are stuck when we sending data for our client (giving error CX_SETD_HTTP_403). I opened a ticket with SAP support but no luck till now.

I have few question to experts if anyone can help here.

1. How to find version of sap ETD( SAP enterprise threat detection) in sap NetWeaver 758 SP01 as it integrated with NW 758

2. On SAP system ETD launchpad is (version 0.3). Do we need to upgrade it on NetWeaver 758 SP01.

3. Any suggestion to resolve above error?

saludos

entry.title: Re: Mass Upload of Equipment Classification & Characteristics Data Using LTMC in SAP
entry.content:
Hi Siva,

very nice and informative blog. Just one comment. If you are using the Migration Cockpit app, then you should not use the term LTMC in title or text. LTMC is a transaction code and that only refers to the old web-dynpro app that is retired since 2021. The new MC app has from a UI perspective nothing in common with the old transaction and also old LTMC projects cannot be used anymore with the new app. 

It is also useful to name the release where you did the data migration esp. as objects are often delivered with new versions and the structures and available fields vary between the releases.

Here also the link for the migration object documentation with additional information that might be helpful for your readers: Object classification - General template | SAP Help Portal

Thanks and BR 🙂

Frank

entry.title: Re: Joule Studio: From Business Intent to Enterprise-Ready AI Agents

@Swetha__Balusamy 
There are two products:

- SAP Build with Joule Studio (Skills, Agents)  announced end of 2025
- Joule Studio which is NEW since Sapphire announced during Sapphire 2026.

Those are 2 different products. 

To get access to (new) Joule Studio:
- you can try to join EAC program
- get access to the Trail once it will be available

entry.title: Re: Variable Pay: how to correctly configure field format...

entry.title: Re: Guidance on growth and lifecycle of BW table /BIC/AZA...
entry.content:
Hi

basically

the suffix /BIC/ means  in BW CUSTOM development -->/BI0/ means standard SAP  standard business content. 

A --> adso Z-->   custom ADSO - so your BW datastore is  ZADSOPPRS 

the ending number 2 means--> Active table (requests active for reporting)

so you should analyze  ZADSOPPRS  in the BW workbench - (a large growth is usually linked to a FULL dataloads procedure that increases dramatically the number of records in the tables)

Hope it helps

Thanks in advance!

Sree

entry.title: Re: June Developer Challenge - CAP Knowledge - Task 06 - Project specific configuration
AI: Building an AI Chat Application on SAP BTP with OpenAI, MCP Server, and SAPUI5 Frontend
entry.content:
AI is one of the hottest topics in the technology industry today. To explore its capabilities within the SAP ecosystem, I decided to build a simple MCP server with an AI API and deploy the solution on SAP BTP.

MCP acts as a lightweight bridge layer between the SAPUI5 frontend and the OpenAI API. Instead of calling OpenAI directly from the UI, all requests are routed through the MCP server. This approach improves security, keeps the API key hidden on the backend, and allows us to easily extend the logic later with SAP-specific processing, logging, or integration with SAP BTP services.

This example uses OpenAI for simplicity. In enterprise scenarios, the same architectural approach can be adapted to SAP AI services or other LLM providers.

The system architecture is decomposed as follows:

  • AI API (LLM)
  • MCP server
  • SAP BTP application
  • SAPUI5 frontend

 

1) AI API

For the AI API, I chose the OpenAI LLM API. To access the API, a subscription is required. However, the same approach can be applied to any other LLM provider by implementing a similar layer.

Open the API keys menu and create a new key. You should securely store the secret key for later use, as it will not be shown again.

 

2) SAP BTP Platform

The next step is setting up the platform for the project.

Create a new SAP BTP Trial instance.
Here are the guidelines for account creation:
https://developers.sap.com/tutorials/hcp-create-trial-account..html

At this point, we have all the necessary components for development.

We have SAP BTP instance, Cloud Foundry environment: API endpoint, Org name, Org ID and SAP Business application studio.

 

3) MCP server setup

The next step is the MCP server setup.

Make sure that the Cloud Foundry CLI is installed.
If not, you can refer to the following resources:

https://help.sap.com/docs/btp/sap-business-technology-platform/download-and-install-cloud-foundry-command-line-interface
https://github.com/cloudfoundry/cli#downloads

Visual Studio Code is used for development.

Create a folder called MCP-Server and add the following files:

MCP-SERVER
- manifest.yml
- mta.yml
- package.json
- server.js
- xs-security.json

The source code is as follows:

manifest.json

In the manifest file, the OpenAI API key can be specified, but it is recommended to use Cloud Foundry commands instead for better security:

Push the environment variable:

cf set-env mcp-server OPENAI_API_KEY "sk-proj-*****"

and restart the mcp server:

cf restage mcp-server

 

applications:
  - name: mcp-server
    memory: 256M
    buildpacks:
      - nodejs_buildpack
    command: npm start
    random-route: true
    env:
      OPENAI_API_KEY: "sk-proj-…"

 mta.yml

_schema-version: "3.3.0"
ID: mcp-server
version: 1.0.0

modules:
  - name: mcp-server
    type: nodejs
    path: .
    parameters:
      memory: 256M
    requires:
      - name: mcp-xsuaa
    properties:
      OPENAI_API_KEY: "YOUR_KEY_HERE"

resources:
  - name: mcp-xsuaa
    type: org.cloudfoundry.managed-service
    parameters:
      service: xsuaa
      service-plan: application

package.json

{
  "name": "mcp-server",
  "version": "1.0.0",
  "scripts": {
    "start": "node server.js"
  },
  "dependencies": {
    "express": "^4.19.2",
    "openai": "^4.0.0",
    "@sap/xsenv": "^3.4.0"
  }
}

Server.js

Here I define that the AI bot should act as an SAP ABAP RAP expert, so I set the appropriate instructions:

const express = require("express");
const xsenv = require("@sap/xsenv");
const OpenAI = require("openai");

const app = express();
app.use(express.json());

/**
 * =========================
 * SAFE XSUAA LOADING
 * =========================
 */
let xsuaa;

try {
  const services = xsenv.getServices({ xsuaa: { tag: "xsuaa" } });
  xsuaa = services.xsuaa;
  console.log("XSUAA found");
} catch (e) {
  console.log("XSUAA not bound - running without auth");
}

/**
 * =========================
 * HEALTH CHECK (IMPORTANT)
 * =========================
 */
app.get("/health", (req, res) => {
  res.json({
    status: "OK",
    xsuaa: !!xsuaa
  });
});

/**
 * =========================
 * OPENAI CLIENT
 * =========================
 */
const client = new OpenAI({
  apiKey: process.env.OPENAI_API_KEY
});

/**
 * =========================
 * MCP ENDPOINT
 * =========================
 */
app.post("/mcp/chat", async (req, res) => {
  try {
    const { prompt } = req.body;

    if (!prompt) {
      return res.status(400).json({ error: "prompt required" });
    }

    const result = await client.chat.completions.create({
      model: "gpt-4.1-mini",
      messages: [
        {
          role: "system",
          content: "You are SAP ABAP RAP debugging assistant."
        },
        {
          role: "user",
          content: prompt
        }
      ]
    });

    res.json({
      answer: result.choices[0].message.content
    });

  } catch (err) {
    console.error(err);
    res.status(500).json({ error: err.message });
  }
});

/**
 * =========================
 * START SERVER (CF SAFE)
 * =========================
 */
const port = process.env.PORT || 3000;

app.listen(port, () => {
  console.log("MCP server running on port", port);
});

xs-security.json

{
  "xsappname": "mcp-server",
  "tenant-mode": "dedicated",
  "scopes": [
    {
      "name": "$XSAPPNAME.invoke",
      "description": "Invoke MCP API"
    }
  ],
  "role-templates": [
    {
      "name": "MCPInvoker",
      "scope-references": [
        "$XSAPPNAME.invoke"
      ]
    }
  ]
}

Next steps are deployment.

Login to the Cloud foundry:

cf login

...and provide all necessary credentials.

Deployment MCP server:

cf push mcp-server -- random route

XSUAA creation:

cf create-service xsuaa application mcp-xsuaa -c xs-security.json

Alternative solution is create XSUAA using SAP BTP and “Authorization and Trust Management Service”.

Binding the application with XSUAA:

cf bind-service mcp-server XSUAA
cf restage mcp-server

For demonstration purposes, the XSUAA configuration was intentionally simplified to make the setup easier to follow.

Checking the apps:

cf apps

Checking the services:

cf services

The server is ready and we can check how it works using PowerShell

Checking the MCP server responces.

The mcp-server URL you may find at SAP BTP at Application overview.

Invoke-RestMethod `                                                                                                                                                                                             
>>   -Uri "https://mcp-server-{...}hana.ondemand.com/health"

Checking the LLM:

Invoke-RestMethod `                 
>>   -Method Post `                                                                              
>>   -Uri "https://mcp-server-{...}.hana.ondemand.com/mcp/chat" `
>>   -Body '{ "prompt": "Explain RAP behavior definition" }' `
>>   -ContentType "application/json"

 

 

4) Frontend

With the MCP server in place, the next step is to build the frontend.

Open SAP Business Application Studio and create a development space.

https://developers.sap.com/tutorials/appstudio-devspace-create..html

Then, generate a basic SAP Fiori application named MCP_CLIENT, using MAIN as the main view.

For simplicity, I will skip the MVC explanation and focus on the implementation of the view and controller and simple one page application.

main.view.xml

<mvc:View
    controllerName="mcpclient.controller.Main"
    xmlns:mvc="sap.ui.core.mvc"
    xmlns="sap.m">

    <Page
        id="mainPage"
        title="MCP Chat">

        <content>

            <List
                id="chatList"
                items="{chat>/messages}"
                growing="true"
                growingScrollToLoad="true"
                class="chatList">

                <CustomListItem id="chatListItem">

                    <HBox
                        id="chatHBox"
                        width="100%">

                        <VBox
                            id="chatVBox"
                            class="{chat>bubbleClass}"
                            width="70%">

                            <Text
                                id="roleText"
                                text="{chat>role}"
                                class="roleText" />

                            <Text
                                id="messageText"
                                text="{chat>text}"
                                wrapping="true"
                                class="messageText" />

                        </VBox>

                    </HBox>

                </CustomListItem>

            </List>

        </content>

        <footer>

            <Toolbar id="chatToolbar">

                <TextArea
                    id="promptInput"
                    width="100%"
                    rows="2"
                    placeholder="Ask MCP server..." />

                <Button
                    id="sendButton"
                    text="Send"
                    type="Emphasized"
                    press=".onSend" />

            </Toolbar>

        </footer>

    </Page>

</mvc:View>

main.controller.js

sap.ui.define([
    "sap/ui/core/mvc/Controller",
    "sap/ui/model/json/JSONModel",
    "sap/m/MessageToast"
], function (Controller, JSONModel, MessageToast) {
    "use strict";

    return Controller.extend("mcpclient.controller.Main", {

        onInit: function () {
            this.getView().setModel(new JSONModel({
                messages: []
            }), "chat");
        },

        onSend: async function () {

            const oView = this.getView();
            const oModel = oView.getModel("chat");
            const oInput = oView.byId("promptInput");

            const sPrompt = (oInput.getValue() || "").trim();

            if (!sPrompt) {
                MessageToast.show("Enter prompt");
                return;
            }

            const aMessages = oModel.getProperty("/messages") || [];

            // 👉 add user message
            aMessages.push({
                role: "USER",
                text: sPrompt
            });

            oModel.setProperty("/messages", aMessages);
            oInput.setValue("");

            this.getView().setBusy(true);

            try {

                // 🔥 SINGLE SOURCE OF TRUTH
                const response = await fetch("/mcp/chat", {
                    method: "POST",
                    headers: {
                        "Content-Type": "application/json"
                    },
                    body: JSON.stringify({
                        prompt: sPrompt
                    })
                });

                const rawText = await response.text();

                if (!response.ok) {
                    throw new Error(`HTTP ${response.status}: ${rawText}`);
                }

                let data;
                try {
                    data = JSON.parse(rawText);
                } catch (e) {
                    data = { answer: rawText };
                }

                const answer =
                    data.answer ||
                    data.response ||
                    data.text ||
                    rawText;

                // 👉 add MCP message
                aMessages.push({
                    role: "MCP",
                    text: answer
                });

                oModel.setProperty("/messages", aMessages);

            } catch (e) {

                aMessages.push({
                    role: "MCP",
                    text: "Error: " + (e.message || e)
                });

                oModel.setProperty("/messages", aMessages);

                MessageToast.show("MCP call failed");
            }

            this.getView().setBusy(false);
        }

    });

});

style.css

.chatList {
    padding: 10px;
}

.userBubble {
    background: #0070f2;
    color: white;
    padding: 10px;
    border-radius: 12px;
    margin: 5px;
}

.mcpBubble {
    background: #eaecee;
    color: #1d2d3e;
    padding: 10px;
    border-radius: 12px;
    margin: 5px;
}

.roleText {
    font-size: 11px;
    opacity: 0.7;
    margin-bottom: 4px;
}

.messageText {
    white-space: pre-wrap;
    word-break: break-word;
}

To avoid CORS issues, the ui5.yaml should look as follows:

specVersion: "4.0"
metadata:
  name: mcpclient

type: application

server:
  customMiddleware:
    - name: fiori-tools-proxy
      afterMiddleware: compression
      configuration:
        ignoreCertErrors: true

        ui5:
          path:
            - /resources
            - /test-resources
          url: https://ui5.sap.com

        backend:
          - path: /mcp
            url: https://mcp-server-{.......}.hana.ondemand.com

Let’s start the application.

Users can enter questions and receive responses from the LLM. As expected, the responses are mainly focused on ABAP RAP, as defined in the MCP server configuration.

 

5) Conclusion

This project demonstrates how modern AI capabilities can be integrated into the SAP ecosystem using SAP BTP and Cloud Foundry.

What we achieved:

  • Connected a SAPUI5 frontend with an AI-powered backend
  • Introduced an MCP server as a bridge between SAP and the OpenAI API
  • Built a foundation for an intelligent SAP assistant

This solution can be extended in multiple directions to evolve into an enterprise-ready AI assistant within the SAP landscape. For example, the MCP server can be enhanced into an SAP ABAP RAP assistant, capable of generating or explaining RAP business objects and service definitions. It can also be integrated with SAP backend systems, enabling interaction with real business data such as orders, materials, or finance objects.

From a broader perspective, this is not just a prototype, but a starting point for building a real SAP AI assistant that understands business context, ABAP RAP patterns, and system-specific knowledge.

#B2BExchange

entry.title: Re: UII Not Populating During LTMC Inventory Upload
entry.content:
Hi

the migration object Material inventory balance | SAP Help Portal does not support key-user-extensibility. To support key-user-extensibility the API that is used for data migration must be registered to support this. The used API is not registered for this.  BAPIs need special coding to support the new key-user-extensibility. EXTENSIONIN parameters are not compatible with the migration cockpit. So, enhancing the BAPI_GOODSMVT_CREATE with new EXTENSIONIN parameters will not help here, as you cannot really use them within the migration cockpit. 

What release are you working on? As you are using the retired transaction name LTMC I guess you are below SAP S/4HANA 2021 release. The term LTMC is only being used for the old web-dynpro based transaction LTMC. Using it for releases after 2020 may confuse people.

Dependent on the release you are working the object may also differ. Since release 1909 the new SIF_INVENTORY_2  replaces the old SIF_INVENTORYBAL object.

R_GOODSMVT_SERIALNUM-UII field is currently not supported within standard field mapping.

The serial number structure R_GOODSMVT_SERIALNUM is also not filled via field mapping. The fields are filled using Application insert within an event rules. So, you must check the  object related event rule EOR_GMVT_ITEM for the specific coding to map serialn umber fields and may adapt that coding. Copy the existing object and then copy the existing rule to an own rule and then make adaptions within that new rule.

If the BAPI supports UII field, then it should be possible to modify the object in a way that this field is mapped from a sender structure field to the target structure field within EOR_GMVT_ITEM.  You must also check the SAP Notes for BAPI_GOODSMVT_CREATE regarding issues for UII field usage if you need to also implement some error corrections for the BAPI.

BR

Frank

entry.title: Re: How to Implement SAP Business One projects successfully
entry.content:
Great breakdown on the implementation steps. Having run 20+ SAP B1 go-lives across Hyderabad's pharma and manufacturing sector, I'd add one critical nuance: the go-live week is where SMBs lose the most money — and it's almost never a technical issue.

Here's the checklist our team uses, built from hard lessons:

Data Readiness (2 weeks before go-live)

  • Run parallel inventory count in legacy system AND SAP B1. Any variance >2% = stop and resolve before cutover.
  • Validate UoM conversions across all item masters — this is the single most common post-go-live data disaster.
  • Freeze master data 48 hours before cutover night. No new items, vendors, or customers.

Real-Time Visibility Setup (before go-live)

  • Configure your key KPI dashboards before Day 1 — inventory levels, open orders, cashflow position. If your team can't see these in real time on Day 1, adoption collapses.
  • Identify which reports your finance head runs every morning. Map them in SAP B1 before go-live — not after.
  • For pharma shops: batch cost visibility per production order should be live on Day 1, not Week 3. If you're running WHO-GMP or Schedule M ops, this isn't optional.

User Readiness

  • Identify 2–3 power users per department in Week 1 of the project. Train them deeply — they're your go-live support army.
  • Test: can every user process their core daily transaction end-to-end in SAP B1 without consultant help? If no, go-live is premature.

Cutover Readiness

  • 5-day parallel run — process every real transaction in BOTH systems simultaneously. Painful. Necessary.
  • Document the rollback decision tree: who decides, under what conditions, and what's the recovery plan. Teams that skip this average 3–4 weeks of post-go-live chaos vs. 3–4 days for teams that have it.
  • Go-live support team available for 72 hours straight: internal champion + consultant.
  • Finance runs first month-end close in SAP B1 within 5 business days of go-live.

One thing I'd add beyond the standard checklist: modern implementations should plan for AI-assisted operations visibility from Day 1. The question isn't just "can we process transactions in SAP B1" — it's "can our MD and CFO see live cashflow forecasts, inventory KPIs, and supplier quality data without pulling a report?" If the answer is no, you've implemented SAP B1 but you haven't captured its full value.

What specific module are you struggling with? Happy to go deeper.

— Venkata Siva Reddy Polu, TEKROI
SAP B1 implementation for Hyderabad pharma and manufacturing SMBs

entry.title: Initiate ONB 2.0 for someone with the same name but is not the same person
entry.content:
We recently "upgraded" from SF ONB 1.0 to 2.0. We need to know how to initiate ONB 2.0 for someone with the same name but is not the same person. We currently have this scenario. Employee Joe Smith is a former employee, employee Joe Smith is a new hire (not the same person) starting soon. When we initiate ONB 2.0 for new hire Joe Smith, we get a Profile Match Verification for Joe Smith, but this is not the same person. Do we just click on "Proceed as New Hire"? 

To be clear, this is not a re-hire, this is a new hire with the same name.

entry.title: Re: From XSUAA to AMS: Architecting S4HANA CAP Extensions with Policy-Based Authorization
entry.content:
Hello @Avinash_Vaidya ,
Yes, this CAP perspective is what I understood from your blog. But since you seem to be from SAP, and since you are, in a way, "encouraging" developers to adopt AMS in certain use-cases, I was wondering where Security Admins can find the next part of this story, not in SAP Cloud Identity Services, we all know where it lies, but in a blog post that would help us understand how to handle thousands of users when it comes to (de)provisioning policies. Last I checked, there was no decent tools in AMS to address basic user maintenance. So, I can't wait to see something published on this topic. Do not hesitate to convey this message to your colleagues expert in SAP Cloud Identity Services.
Thanks
Stéphan

entry.title: Sap Analytics Cloud - Dimension transformation calculated column not populating with right values
entry.content:
Hi

I am facing two  issues with dimension transformation - calculated column while importing flat file upload 

1. calculated column name  =  IF([property1] ="MIXED" ,CONCAT("Other ", [property2])," ")

I am expecting if property1 is not "MIXED" , it will populate blanks and it is showing correct values after writing the formula but  import job populating property2 values after finish mapping . 

2. after some point of time, it is not showing calculated column as property in my dimension sheet.  I have refreshed sheet, browser refreshed, logged out and login . But all are unsuccessful. Even formula it is showing in Query transformation too , but is it not attaching to dimension sheet.

Any suggestions?

 

regards

Mahesh

entry.title: Re: June Developer Challenge - CAP Knowledge - Task 04 - Namespaces, contexts and name prefixes

Some time ago, I converted all my dashboards from the classic design experience to the optimized design experience. In addition, I also converted the “Flexible Pages.”

Now I’m wondering how I can switch to the new table experience. In my DC33 instance, I unfortunately do not receive any corresponding popup. Also, there is no option in the lower section of the right-hand builder panel to manually convert individual dashboards.

Is this behavior expected at the moment, or am I perhaps being a bit too impatient and the feature will only be enabled at a later stage? 😁

btw.:

Additionally, when creating a new dashboard using the optimized design experience, the table is currently not provided in the new table experience, nor can it be switched manually.

Thanks a lot and best regards,
Jürgen.

entry.title: Re: TDT and RITA Configuration Check results in inconsist...
entry.content:
Hi @maheshmohan,

The tag "SAP Readiness Check" relates to the transformation toolset documented here in the SAP Help Portal: https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_READINESS_CHECK. 

The issue you describe below is related to the Test Data Refresh system preparation check. We will discuss the name of their check with them to help avoid confusion in the future. In the meantime, I suggest creating a support case under the component CA-DT-TDR to address your issue.

Best Regards,
Greg

entry.title: Re: Will deprecation of Learning Token Server change the...

entry.title: Re: Why SAP S/4HANA Transformations Fail When Business Processes Are Automated Before They Are Optim
entry.content:
I am generally aligned, however, I would say that the Best Practices SAP provides should always be the starting point. Then the question should be "why won't this work?" or "what are the gaps?" rather than generically, "how can we improve the process?". 

Process improvement from an outside perspective assumes the customer has awareness that the process is in need of improvement and can articulate what the improvements need to be. At most companies, if they knew, it would already be getting adjusted. By using SAP Best Practices as a starting point, and then challenging with what are the gaps in Best Practices, you change the framing of the process discussion.

On another note, in the next 3 to 5 years it may not matter much. As customers learn to use AI more effectively it will radically reduce the reliance on consultants, coders, offshore support, etc. It is all going to radically change. The capability is there right now but the techniques for using AI in that way are certainly not being pushed by the vast majority of SIs. Everyone in professional services is avoiding the creative destruction that is coming. It is right around the corner.

entry.title: Re: Employee as BP
entry.content: You may want to look into the HR Mini-Master. It can be deployed for exactly this scenario and without setting up all of HCM/HR. It was originally designed specifically for this purpose. There are even IMG nodes in SD for setting up the sales reps in this way.

entry.title: Re: corsfilter on /medias
entry.content: I have added the corsfilter.mediaweb to no effect. I don't see corsfilter referenced like in other extensions.

entry.title: Transaction handling in CAP within try-catch and a loop
entry.content:
How does CAP handle DB errors that happen inside of try-catch clauses inside of a loop?

Below is my sample code. Assume only order #2 fails when updating "Users" and catch engages.

What will be saved?

What I observed in our real application (when we got a deadlock due to concurrent update to "Users"): book "b2" and user "u2" wouldn't be updated and catch was engaged. But then order "2" would be updated (obviously because it happens after the first loop). As if each try-catch would have its own transaction scope. It looked like all other objects (from order 1 and order 3) were updated too.

When I tested in local environment with SQLite -> the catch did not engage, everything ran without exceptions but then the whole request would fail with 500 and nothing would be saved. As if it remembers that the whole request is poisoned and fails in the very end. I assume handling is different when using HANA. I must mention that locally I simulated error differently (by trying to set the ID to an already used value). I am not sure how to simulate concurrent writes from multiple instances in a local environment...

 

this.on('test', async (req) => {

  const goodOrders = [];
  const badOrders = [];
  for (const order of req.data.value) {
    try {
      await cds.update('Books', order.bookID).with({ stock: { '+=': -1 } });
      goodOrders.push(order);
      // Assume "deadlock exception" for user "u2" from order "2".
      await cds.update('Users', order.userID).with({ totalBooks: { '+=': 1 } });
    } catch (error) {
      badOrders.push(order);
    }
  }

  for (const goodOrder of goodOrders) {
    await cds.update('Orders', goodOrder).with({ state: 'OK' });
  }

  return {
    goodOrders,
    badOrders,
  }
})

POST /examples:

{
  "value": [
    { "ID": "1", "bookID": "b1", "userID": "u1" },
    { "ID": "2", "bookID": "b2", "userID": "u2-error!!!" },
    { "ID": "3", "bookID": "b3", "userID": "u3"}
  ]
}

 I would appreciate any links to documentation. The CAP doc is quite sparse around transaction handling...

entry.title: Re: System temporarily unavailable error - C_THR81_2512 launch - Support closing tickets without res
entry.content: It just sat on the same screen saying 'something went wrong' for an hour and cancelled itself. Seems we cannot rebook until new exam is in on June 17 or 18.

entry.title: Part 1–Business process Credit Risk-Based Impairment in SAP S/4HANA
entry.content:
During a recent SAP S/4HANA project implementation, I worked on Credit Risk Based Impairment in compliance with IFRS9, While the Concept is Expected credit loss(ECL) from accounting perspective. Understanding how SAP handles ECL Posting , delta calculation and Write off customer balance.

This blog series shares end-to-end business process based on that implementation experience . 

  • First Part, business concept and process flow using a practical example. 
  • Second Part, will cover the configuration Steps.

 IFRS-9: Companies must record Provisions and expense related to Expected credit loss for Financial Asset and Accounts receivables before the actual loss occurs due to customer insolvency. The main Goal of ECL is to provide a realistic and predictive view of financial risk by on the estimating losses in advance. this helps companies to improve the financial transparency comply with international financial standard.

Expected credit loss ECL=Probability of default (POD) x Loss Given Default(LGD) x Exposure at default(EAD)

The business process in SAP

SAP Business Process Flow

As shown in the process flow, SAP S4/HANA applies a full business Credit risk based impairment cycle in compliance with IFRS9

  1. Identify the risk class in the customer master data (Low, medium, High).
  2. The AR Department records customer open invoices, and it's updated in both the Customer account and general ledger reconciliation account.
  3. The Finance team starts to calculate the ECL based on the risk class, POD% and outstanding customer open item (the calculation based on open item level) ECL=outstanding amount * POD%.

SAP automatically Posts the ECL expense account (P&L Account) and the Provision is posed as a negative amount in the main general ledger reconciliation account (Balance sheet account).

Loss Allowance = Probability of Default (PD) * Open Amount

Probability of Default are defined based on:

  1. Aging increments of the open items
  2. Risk Class of the business Partner

Mapping aging increments to risk classes and their corresponding Probability of Default (PD%).(Part 2 Configuration Step) Mapping aging increment to risk classes and corresponding Probability of Default (PD%)

Define Aging Increments for Advanced Valuation. (Part 2 Configuration Step)Aging Increments

The longer outstanding amount remains open, the higher customer’s risk class becomes, which increases the level of Probability of default, This results in an increase of provision calculation and impact on P&L statement.

Key Features

You only have to set up profit and loss (P&L) accounts. You don't need any reconciliation accounts for your balance sheet accounts. Postings are always made to the account to be valuated. Postings are made directly to the reconciliation accounts.

Post Credit-Risk-Based Impairment integrates the data of the Write Off Receivables app. When you've used the Write Off Receivables app, to write off receivables, you carry out Post Credit-Risk-Based Impairment as the next step. Credit-risk-based impairment recognizes and corrects the write-off postings and thus avoids wrong P&L and balance sheet results.

That means that you always have to carry out Post Credit-Risk-Based Impairment after you've done the last write-off in the Write Off Receivables app.

The business process starts with the following steps :

A) Master Data Setup – Risk Class Assignment
Based on our implementation experience, careful validation of customer risk classifications is essential before running the impairment process. Since the Probability of Default is derived from the assigned risk class, data quality in the business partner master record plays an important role in achieving accurate ECL results. So, The business process starts with identifying the risk class in customer master data.

In app-maintained business partner, under the General tab ”Credit Profile”, The risk class (Low, Medium, High).

BP Master Data

B) Customer Invoice Posting (Open Item Creation)

Customer outstanding open amount by 1 Million SAR on 01.04.2026, which creates an open item in Accounts Receivable and is also reflected in the General Ledger reconciliation account.

Customer Open Item

C) ECL Calculation – Post Credit-Risk-Based Impairment Run

One interesting behavior we observed during testing was that SAP calculates the impairment at the open-item level rather than at the customer balance level. This provides more accurate results, especially when customers have invoices with different aging periods. So in our example during executed via Schedule General ledger Jobs:

On 01.07.2026, Which is the valuation key date. Since the invoice is overdue by 90 days, the system applies the configured risk rules, for the risk class D (High Default Risk), a probability of default (POD)% of 60% is applied.

Resulting in an Expected Credit loss (ECL) of 600,000 (1,000,000 x 60%). the system posts the entry as:

Dr ECL Expense 600,000
             Cr Reconciliation Account 600,000

In the Schedule General Ledger Jobs app, select Post Credit-Risk-Based Impairment template to initiate the impairment calculation process.

Schedule General Ledger Jobs APP

Enter the required parameter for  Company Code 1000 and Leading Ledger 0L, with a valuation key date of 01.07.2026 and select Update run. Based on these parameters, SAP analyzes the outstanding receivables and determines the applicable impairment amount.scheduled General Ledger jobs

SAP calculates an Expected Credit Loss (ECL) of 600,000 SAR, based on an outstanding receivable of 1,000,000 SAR and a Probability of Default (PD) of 60%.scheduled General Ledger jobs

Customer Reconciliation GL Account is credited by 600,000 SAR, reducing the outstanding receivable balance after the impairment calculation posted.Receivables Recon GL Account

Note :
A common misunderstanding that  caused some confusion was the way SAP handles subsequent impairment runs. Rather than reposting the full impairment amount each time, the system only posts the additional amount needed to reach the new expected credit loss value. This delta approach helps prevent duplicate postings and keeps the financial results accurate. 

On the 31.10.2026 The outstanding customer amount has not paid, it’s overdue by 120 days and less than 1 year. At this stage, the risk increases so the Probability of Default (POD) rises from 60% to 80%.  As a result, the total expected credit loss (ECL) becomes 800,000 SAR. since the system already recognized 600,000 SAP in the previous stage run. The system will post only additional (delta) of 200,000 SAR as extra provision in the current period.

In the Schedule General Ledger Jobs app, select the Post Credit-Risk-Based Impairment template and enter the required parameter for  Company Code 1000 and Leading Ledger 0L, with a valuation key date of 31.10.2026 and select Update run. Based on these parameters, SAP analyzes the outstanding receivables and determines the applicable impairment amount.

scheduled General Ledger jobs

SAP calculates an Expected Credit Loss (ECL)  becomes 800,000 SAR  due to the POD% increase from 60% to 80%. since the system already recognized 600,000 SAP in the previous stage run. The system will post only additional (delta) of 200,000 SAR as extra provision in the current period.scheduled General Ledger jobs

SAP posts an additional impairment of 200,000 SAR to the Customer Reconciliation GL Account. This increases the total provision to 800,000 SAR, leaving a net receivable balance of 200,000 SAR.

Receivables Recon GL Account

D) Customer Write-Off Processing

On 15.12.2026. it’s Confirmed that the customer has gone bankrupt and will not make any payment, therefore in this stage the remaining balance must be fully written off. The AR department uses the Write Off Receivables app to clear the outstanding customer open amount and record the final loss in the system.

Open Write Off Receivables ID F6728

Write Off Receivables ID F6728

After selecting the customer account, SAP displays all open receivable items. Select the outstanding invoice identified for write-off and choose Post to complete the write-off process and record the transaction in the system.

Write Off Receivables ID F6728

Financial Posting: Debit ECL Expense G/L Account and Credit Customer Account (ABC).

Write Off Financial Impact

E) Final Impairment Run – Revaluation & Clearing

During testing, we notices that the Credit-Risk-Based Impairment run must be executed after the final write-off transaction. Otherwise, impairment balances may remain in the reconciliation account and require additional adjustments. Therefore, the previously recognized ECL amount of 800,000 SAR is reversed, ensuring that all balances are fully cleared and updated correctly.

The open item amount for the customer becomes zero against the full Expected Credit Loss expense amounting to 1 million SAR.

 

The Post Credit-Risk-Based Impairment job is executed again using the Schedule General Ledger Jobs app. The system reverses the previously recognized impairment amount.

Scheduled General Ledger jobs

Customer Reconciliation G/L Account is fully cleared after the Credit-Risk-Based Impairment run. SAP reverses the previously recognized impairment postings, ensuring that the reconciliation account balance is correctly aligned following the customer write-off.

Receivables Recon GL Account

The ECL Expense G/L Account is fully cleared through the impairment revaluation run. SAP reverses the previously recognized impairment postings and retains only the final write-off amount.

ECL (P&L) GL Account

Conclusion

SAP S/4HANA automates the Credit Risk Based Impairment process in compliance with IFRS9 by assessing customer risk classification ,probability of default percentage and aging analysis. Starts with outstanding invoice until the balance is written off.

This end to end process helps organizations maintain accurate balance sheet and P&L reporting while improving visibility into customer credit risk. 

In Part 2, I will explain the configuration steps by step and provide detailed system setup.
Stay tuned and follow for the next part of the series.

Thank you.

entry.title: Re: Introducing Our New Reply Progression Badges and a Color Refresh!
entry.content: glad you had a chance to weigh in @M-K ! And love the Holy Grail rmovie eference @JimSpath ! One of my favorites.

entry.title: Re: Introducing Our New Reply Progression Badges and a Color Refresh!
entry.content:
The votes are in and I’m excited to announce our winning colors!

Teal, Mango, Green, Indigo and Raspberry

A big thank you to everyone who took the time to share their favorite colors. It was great to see such strong engagement and thoughtful preferences across the community!

We explored a few different ways of counting votes (including ranked and normalized approaches), and the outcome was consistent across all methods, which gives us  confidence that these truly reflect the community’s preferences.

These colors will be making their way into the badge experience as part of the refresh. Stay tuned as they roll out!

Thanks again for helping shape this, this is the kind of community input that makes these updates more meaningful.

entry.title: Re: SAP S/4HANA 2025 Fully-Activated Appliance: Known Issues
entry.content:
Hi Joerg,

my bad and i've obviously misunderstood the instructions and never locked/unlocked individual users in sapgui. found the menu option where clicking on the (unopened?) lock actually does unlock the user. in webgui there is a lock button that would invoke it, anyway. su10 is more obvious, even when used for a single username.

thanks for the tip.

greg

entry.title: Re: Existing Formation is Missing in Portfolio & Products...
entry.content:
Dear customer,

It's noticed that you have also submitted customer case under component XX-SER-FORME, and the case has reached the SAP for Me development team responsible. 

The developer is internally checking the issue further for you. Could you pls kindly check further and wait for the update via the customer case?

Thanks and regards,
SAP Support

entry.title: BPマスタのFI得意先タブにある「振込人名」の表示順について
entry.content:
お世話になっております。
BPマスタのFI得意先タブにある「振込人名」の表示順について、SAP標準仕様を確認させてください。
現在、振込人名の入力項目は、以下のように「先頭数字」と「振込人名」の2つの要素で構成されている認識です。
例：
1　振込人名111
2　振込人名222
3　振込人名333
当初、複数の振込人名を登録した場合、表示順は先頭数字の昇順になると想定しておりました。
想定していた表示順：
1　振込人名111
2　振込人名222
3　振込人名333
しかし、実機で確認したところ、表示順は先頭数字の順番ではなく、入力・保存した順番で表示されているように見受けられます。
例えば、以下の順番で振込人名を入力・保存した場合、表示結果は下記の通りとなりました。
【確認例】
「3　333」を入力して保存表示結果：
3　333
続けて「2　222」を入力して保存表示結果：
3　333
2　222
続けて「1　111」を入力して保存表示結果：
3　333
2　222
1　111
続けて「4　444」を入力して保存表示結果：
3　333
2　222
1　111
4　444
上記の結果より、振込人名の表示順は、先頭数字の昇順ではなく、入力順または登録順に従って表示されているように見えます。
つきましては、以下についてご確認をお願いいたします。
1. BPマスタのFI得意先タブにおける振込人名の表示順は、「先頭数字」はソートキーではなく、SAP標準仕様として「入力順／登録順」で表示される認識で正しいでしょうか。

お手数をおかけしますが、ご確認のほどよろしくお願いいたします。

entry.title: how to apply download S4 hana cloud ES all data
entry.content:
we already migrated system from S4hana cloud ES to PE, and ES will stop service in coming month, we want to download all bussiness include master data and documents data from system. how to start this ?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: how to apply download S4 hana cloud ES all data
entry.content:
Dear Sender:

To extract and download system data, you can use the app "Customer Data Return".

To execute Customer Data Return, you need to:

  1. Trigger file preparation: This is carried out by the extraction user.

    For more information, see Working with the Customer Data Return - Extraction App.

  2. Trigger file download: This is carried out by the provisioning user.

    For more information, see Working with the Customer Data Return - Provisioning App.

You can find more information in the Help Portal below with this app:

Customer Data Return | SAP Help Portal

entry.content: @Jennifer_Wilson1 yes please proceed as new hire.

entry.title: Re: I am unable to delete BP relationships 33099736 and 3...
entry.content:
Dear customer,

Thanks for the information provided so far but sorry that it's not SAP for Me related issue. Also SAP Community is a preferred Support Channel for How-To Questions (refer to KBA 2973358).

For your specific issue, could you pls kindly create a support case using component LO-MD-BP? Then the expert in that component will review your scenario and assist further. 

Thanks for your cooperation and understanding here.

Best regards,
SAP Support

entry.title: Re: BTP IntegrationSuite SalesfrocePubSub adapter limits/...

entry.title: Anlegen von OSS Meldungen für SF
entry.content:
Der Testuser SPHONE_NUMBER hat das Berechtigungspaket Standard User (intern) und Einzelberechtigungen für das Anlegen von OSS Meldungen für Success Factor. Als Einzelberechtigung ist nicht 'Diplay Cloud Data' auswählbar. 

Wir wollen nicht die Cloud-Administrator PERSON_NAME zuweisen, da diese User kleine Cloud Administratoren sein sollen, sondern ausschließlich berechtigt sein sollen, um OSS Meldung für SF anzulegen.

 


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Anlegen von OSS Meldungen für SF
entry.content:
Regarding your issue, according to the translation, I understand you have an issue that could not assign "display cloud data " authorization on SF installation to S-user *.

As I checked in our backend, the SF installation is *.

The core logic of SAP for Me User Management is that an administrator can only manage authorizations they themselves hold.
Regarding this issue, please verify whether the admin S‑user you are currently using possesses the Display Cloud Data authorization on installation *.
Below is the list of admin users within your company who currently hold this authorization. You may reach out to them to grant the required access to test S‑user *.

entry.title: Re: EWM Product has Block Profile E2 Warning but it is st...
entry.content:
Hi,

it sounds like a bug please a ticket to SAP support team.

Best regards.
Chris

entry.title: Re: Physical Inventory Progress showing all activity areas
entry.content:
Hi, It should be correct behavior. 
Since the product only stored in one activity area which mean other AA should didn't contain any stock and it's not required for PI. 

Best regards,
Chris
